rationale: The citing paper’s core design and analyses are choice-based conjoint with HB logit/consider-then-choose models about scale-driven discriminability effects, while the cited paper develops a different elicitation method (adaptive self-explicated conjoint, ASE) for many-attribute measurement. In the provided evidence, there is only a bare DOI-level reference match (no in-text citation context or signs the ASE procedure was adopted, compared, or used as a mechanism source). This looks most like a general background/methods-literature mention rather than substantive dependence.
